description: Deterministic AHK v2 micro-benchmark. Each task asks a model for an AutoHotkey v2 function named Solve() that returns a single value. A driver prints String(Solve()) to stdout; the harness runs it via CloudAHK and compares stdout (trimmed) to `expected`. Tier 1 covers the universal computation core (algorithms, data structures, string/number work). Tier 2 covers OOP depth, error handling / control flow, and the AHK-specific domain (DllCall, Buffer/NumPut, file I/O, DateDiff) -- where v1-trained models fail hardest.

tasks.probes: Closures / function factories (a returned closure must capture its argument)
tasks.prompt: Write a higher-order function that takes a number `factor` and returns a new function which multiplies its own argument by that captured `factor`. Use it to build a 'triple' function (factor 3), then return the result of calling triple with 14.

tasks.probes: Recursion and large integer arithmetic
tasks.prompt: Compute Fibonacci numbers where fib(1) = 1 and fib(2) = 1. Return fib(30).

tasks.probes: Sorting an Array (AHK v2 has no built-in Array sort method)
tasks.prompt: Sort the array [5, 3, 8, 1, 9, 2, 7] into ascending order, then return the element at 1-based index 5.

tasks.probes: RegExMatch in a loop with the &outputVar capture and Pos advancing
tasks.prompt: Scan the string "ab12cd345ef6" for every maximal run of digits, convert each run to an integer, and return the sum of those integers.

tasks.probes: Map() used as a counter, then finding the maximum value
tasks.prompt: Count how often each space-separated word occurs in the sentence "the quick brown fox the lazy dog the end", and return the highest occurrence count of any single word.

tasks.probes: Class definition, methods, and an Array used as a stack
tasks.prompt: Define a Stack class with Push and Pop methods backed by an array. Push 10, then 20, then 30; Pop twice; Push 40. Return the value currently on top of the stack without removing it.

tasks.probes: Bitwise operators (&, >>) in a loop
tasks.prompt: Return the number of bits set to 1 in the binary representation of the integer 0xABCDEF.

tasks.probes: While loops and INTEGER division -- AHK v2 '/' is float, '//' is integer
tasks.prompt: Starting from 27, repeatedly apply the Collatz rule: if the current number is even, divide it by two; if it is odd, replace it with three times the number plus one. Count how many steps it takes to reach 1 (the starting value 27 is step 0). Return that step count.

tasks.probes: Variadic parameters (args*) and iterating them
tasks.prompt: Write a variadic function that accepts any number of numeric arguments and returns their total. Return the result of calling it with the arguments 3, 14, 15, 9, 26, 5.

tasks.probes: Format() format specifiers and float division
tasks.prompt: Return the result of formatting the value of 22 divided by 7 to exactly three digits after the decimal point, using AHK v2's Format function.

tasks.probes: Class inheritance: extends, method override, inherited property
tasks.prompt: Define a class Animal whose Speak() method returns the string "generic" and which sets an instance property Legs to 4. Define a class Dog that extends Animal and overrides Speak() to return "woof". Create a Dog, and return its Speak() result concatenated directly with its Legs value (e.g. "woof4").

tasks.probes: Property getter AND setter with transformation
tasks.prompt: Define a class with a single property whose setter stores three times whatever value is assigned, and whose getter returns the stored value. Create an instance, assign 7 to that property, then return the property's value.

tasks.probes: Static class field mutated across instances in __New
tasks.prompt: Define a class that keeps a static counter of how many instances have been created, incrementing it inside __New. Create five instances, then return the value of that static counter.

tasks.probes: throw / try / catch and reading Error.Message
tasks.prompt: Write code that throws an Error with the message "boom", catches it in a try/catch, and returns the caught error's Message.

tasks.probes: Catching a runtime (divide-by-zero) error
tasks.prompt: Inside a try block, evaluate 1 divided by 0 so that it raises a runtime error. Catch that error and return the string "caught".

tasks.probes: Switch / Case statement
tasks.prompt: Using a Switch statement on the integer 3, map 1 to "one", 2 to "two", and 3 to "three". Return the matched string.

tasks.probes: for key, value in Map iteration
tasks.prompt: Create a Map with the pairs a=10, b=20, c=30. Using a for-loop that iterates key and value pairs, sum all the values and return the total.

tasks.probes: DllCall into a Win32 function with typed arguments
tasks.prompt: Use DllCall to invoke the Win32 MulDiv function with the arguments 7, 1000, and 3 (all int), and return its integer result.

tasks.probes: Buffer allocation with NumPut / NumGet round-trip
tasks.prompt: Allocate a Buffer, write the unsigned 32-bit integer 0xDEADBEEF into it at offset 0 with NumPut, then read it back with NumGet as an unsigned 32-bit integer and return that value.

tasks.probes: File I/O: FileAppend then FileRead via A_Temp
tasks.prompt: Write exactly the text "hello" (no trailing newline) to a new temporary file under A_Temp, read the file's contents back, delete the file, and return the contents that were read.

tasks.probes: DateDiff with YYYYMMDD timestamps
tasks.prompt: Using DateDiff, return the number of whole days from 2026-01-01 to 2026-06-05.
